data.title: Adani Enterprises sells stake worth $279 million in AWL Agri Business
data.description:
Indian conglomerate Adani Enterprises' unit sold shares worth 25 billion rupees ($278.9 million) in AWL Agri Business via bulk deals, data from exchanges showed on Friday.

Data from NSE showed that Adani Commodities, a subsidiary of Adani Enterprises, sold 39.5 million shares in AWL Agri for 275.09 rupees apiece, while data from BSE showed that it sold 51.5 million shares for 275 rupees apiece.

That reflects a discount of about 0.6% to Thursday's close.

The unit owned a 20% stake in AWL Agri, as of September 30, representing 259.9 million shares held, exchange data showed.

($1 = 89.6510 Indian rupees)

data.title: TRCS: Improved H2 FY25 results, strong cash, and clear growth strategy despite U.K. rail headwinds
data.description:
Second half FY25 performance improved, with results in line with guidance and recurring revenues up 8%. U.K. rail headwinds persist, but FY26 outlook is unchanged, supported by a strong balance sheet, new contracts, and a focus on organic and international growth.

data.title: Analyst Group: Analyst Group Comment on the Outcome of Irisity’s Rights Issue
data.description:
Irisity AB (“Irisity” or “the Company”) announced, on the 20th of November, the outcome of the fully guaranteed rights issue of 217,157,616 shares, corresponding to gross proceeds of approx. SEK 26.1m before issue costs and set-offs. The rights issue was subscribed to a total of 92.5%, of which 86.2% was subscribed through subscription rights and an additional 6.3% without rights. The remaining 7.5% will be allocated to the guarantor, Stockhorn Capital AB. The total set-off amounts to approx. SEK 16.2m, resulting in net cash proceeds of roughly SEK 9.9m before transaction costs (estimated at approx. SEK 0.5m).

In summary, Analyst Group believes that the solid subscription levels, both including and excluding pre-commitments, demonstrate strong underlying support from the existing shareholder base. Together with the reduced debt position following the set-offs, the improved cash balance, and the Company’s planned measures to enhance collections and lower the OPEX-base, the outcome materially strengthens Irisity’s near-term financial profile and supports the execution of the Company’s ongoing transformation plan.

Read Analyst Group’s comment here

About Analyst Group: One of Sweden's leading equity research boutiques with focus on small and medium-sized listed companies.

Read more about Analyst Group

 

This is a press release from Analyst Group regarding the publication of a comment on Irisity. Readers may assume that Analyst Group has received compensation for making the comment. The Company has not been given an opportunity to influence the parts where Analyst Group has had opinions about the Company, future valuation or anything else that could be considered a subjective assessment.

data.title: Archer Aviation falls after rival Joby sues co
data.description:
** Shares of electric aircraft maker Archer Aviation fall nearly 1.2% to about $6.8

** Rival Joby Aviation on Wednesday sued co in California state for allegedly stealing its trade secrets

** "Joby alleges we used their trade secrets to win a 'deal' with a developer but the reality is that Archer has no deal with this developer," said co's chief legal and strategy officer, Eric Lentell

** Six of eight brokerages rate stock "buy" and two "hold"; their median PT is $13 - data compiled by LSEG

** Including session's moves, shares down 30.3% YTD

Company announcement no. 26-25

Copenhagen, November 21, 2025

Shape Robotics A/S (the "Company") hereby updates its financial calendar.

The Q3 2025 Interim Report, previously scheduled for publication on 21 November 2025, will now be published on 28 November 2025.

data.title: REG - Saxon Weald Cap plc - Board member change
data.description:
RNS Number : 5968I Saxon Weald Capital PLC 21 November 2025  

21 November 2025

Saxon Weald Capital plc

Board changes

Saxon Weald Capital plc, a wholly owned subsidiary of Saxon Weald (a registered Community Benefit Society, Reg. no: 7971), announces that Rachel McGoff has been appointed as Saxon Weald's new Executive Director of Finance & Governance and a Board Member of both Saxon Weald and Saxon Weald Capital plc.  

Rachel joins from the Guinness Partnership where she has been Head of Financial Accounting and Reporting since 2021. Rachel is a qualified accountant (ACA) who originally trained as an auditor with KPMG. She has extensive experience in housing association and charity finance and a strong track record in leading improvements and developing high performing teams. Rachel also has expertise in ESG reporting, and currently co-chairs a social housing sector Sustainable Housing Strategy Group.

Saxon Weald's Chief Executive, Corinna Bishopp, says: "We're excited to welcome Rachel to Saxon Weald. She has a proven track record of delivering strong financial results, whilst being an inspiring leader and strategic thinker.

This appointment comes at a pivotal time for us, as we move into our new three-year strategy. Rachel will help us achieve improved value for money, supporting our investment in new and existing homes, as well as increasing the role we play in our local communities. We look forward to the expertise and fresh perspective she will bring."

Saxon Weald's incoming Executive Director of Finance and Governance, Rachel McGoff, says: "I am delighted to be joining Saxon Weald at this pivotal time as they embark on their new strategic plan. Ensuring strong finances and robust long-term planning will be key to support Saxon Weald in delivering for their local communities. I'm excited to be part of this journey."

Rachel will join Saxon Weald in March 2026.

This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact rns@lseg.com or visit www.rns.com.

RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. For further information about how RNS and the London Stock Exchange use the personal data you provide us, please see our Privacy Policy.  END  BOAPPGPWGUPAGCG

data.title: Airthings ASA - Mandatory notification of trade
data.description:
Reference is made to the stock exchange announcement on 4 November 2025 byAirthings ASA ("Airthings") regarding an unconditional mandatory cash offer(the"Offer") by Firda AS (the "Offeror") to acquire all outstanding shares (the"Shares") in Airthings not already owned by the Offeror.

The Offeror has on 21 November 2025 received acceptance of 8,900 shares ofAirthings at a price of NOK 0.10 per share. In total, acceptances have beenreceived for 173,908 shares. Upon completion of the settlement, the Offerorwillhold 416,744,092 shares, corresponding to approximately 41.70% of Airthings'outstanding shares and voting rights.

A PDMR notice is attached to this announcement.

https://newsweb.oslobors.no/message/660337
